description: This file defines API routes for user management in a FastAPI application, including endpoints for retrieving current user info, listing users, creating new users, and updating existing users, with appropriate role-based access control.

methods.name: UserResponse me(user: dict = Depends(get_current_user))
methods.description: Endpoint to retrieve the profile of the currently authenticated user.

methods.name: list[UserResponse] list_users( limit: int = 100, offset: int = 0, users_repo: UsersRepository = Depends(), _: dict = Depends(require_roles(RoleName.admin)), )
methods.description: Endpoint to list users, accessible only by admin users.

methods.name: UserResponse create_user( payload: UserCreateRequest, users_repo: UsersRepository = Depends(), audits_repo: AuditsRepository = Depends(), actor: dict = Depends(require_roles(RoleName.admin)), )
methods.description: Endpoint to create a new user, restricted to admin users, also logs the creation in audits repository.

methods.name: UserResponse update_user( user_id: str, payload: UserUpdateRequest, users_repo: UsersRepository = Depends(), audits_repo: AuditsRepository = Depends(), actor: dict = Depends(require_roles(RoleName.admin)), )
methods.description: Endpoint to update user details, restricted to admin users, and logs the update in audits repository.

methods.name: dict _user_api(doc: dict)
methods.description: Helper function to format user dictionary for API responses.

ctags.name: create_user
ctags.signature: ( payload: UserCreateRequest, users_repo: UsersRepository = Depends(), audits_repo: AuditsRepository = Depends(), actor: dict = Depends(require_roles(RoleName.admin)), )

ctags.name: list_users
ctags.signature: ( limit: int = 100, offset: int = 0, users_repo: UsersRepository = Depends(), _: dict = Depends(require_roles(RoleName.admin)), )

ctags.name: update_user
ctags.signature: ( user_id: str, payload: UserUpdateRequest, users_repo: UsersRepository = Depends(), audits_repo: AuditsRepository = Depends(), actor: dict = Depends(require_roles(RoleName.admin)), )
